back_translation: Abraham journeyed from there to the region of the Negev, settled between the cities of Kadesh and Shur, and dwelt in the city of Gerar.
Abraham said of Sarah his wife, "She is my younger sister." So Abimelech king of Gerar sent and brought Sarah to him.
God came to Abimelech in a night dream and said to him, "Behold, you shall die because of the woman you have taken, for she is another man's wife."
Now Abimelech had not yet drawn near to her, so he replied, "O Lord, would you destroy even a righteous nation?
Did he himself not say to me, 'She is my younger sister,' and she herself said, 'He is my older brother'? I have done this with an upright heart and clean hands."
Then God said to him in the dream, "Yes, I know that you have done this with an upright heart, and I have kept you from sinning against me. That is why I did not allow you to touch her.
Now restore the man's wife, for he is a prophet; he will pray for you and you shall live. But if you do not return her, know that you shall surely die — you and all who belong to you."
Abimelech rose early in the morning, summoned all his court officials, and recounted all these matters to them; and they were terrified.

Then Abimelech called Abraham and said to him, "What have you done to us? How have I sinned against you, that you have brought such a great sin upon me and my kingdom? You have done to me things that ought not to be done."
And Abimelech said to Abraham, "What did you see, that you did this thing?"
Abraham answered, "Because I thought, 'Surely there is no fear of God in this place, and they will kill me on account of my wife.'
Besides, she truly is my sister, the daughter of my father — but not the daughter of my mother — and she became my wife.
When God caused me to wander from my father's house, I said to her, 'This is the steadfast love you shall show me: in every place where we come, say of me, He is my brother.'"
Abimelech took flocks of sheep-and-goats, herds of cattle, male slaves and female slaves, and gave them to Abraham, and restored Sarah his wife to him.
Abimelech said, "Behold, my land is before you. Settle wherever pleases you."
And to Sarah he said, "Behold, I have given a thousand pieces of silver to your brother. Behold, this is a covering of the eyes for all who are with you. You have been vindicated before everyone."
So Abraham prayed to God, and God healed Abimelech, his wife, and his maidservants, so they could bear children again.
For the LORD had completely closed all wombs in the household of Abimelech because of the matter of Sarah, Abraham's wife.
